quality.rationale: 需多步推导联络与曲率张量，结构可推广至任意维共形平坦时空，解答步骤完整且具理论物理动机

origin.question:
Problem 9.19. Compute the Riemann tensor, Ricci tensor, and scalar curvature of the conformally-flat metric $\mathbf{g}_{\mu 
u} = \mathrm{e}^{2\phi}\eta_{\mu 
u}$ where $\phi = \phi (\mathbf{x}^{\mu})$ is an arbitrary function.

## Solution

Section 9.19. In a conformally flat spacetime the Weyl tensor vanishes, so the Riemann tensor can easily be computed from the Ricci tensor
$$
\mathrm {R}_{\mu \nu} = - \left(\log | \mathrm {g} |^{\frac {1}{2}}\right)_{, \mu \nu} + \Gamma_{\mu \nu , \alpha}^{\alpha} + \left(\log | \mathrm {g} |^{\frac {1}{2}}\right)_{, \alpha} \Gamma_{\mu \nu}^{\alpha} - \Gamma_{\mu \alpha}^{\beta} \Gamma_{\nu \beta}^{\alpha}.
$$
The expressions we need are
$$
\begin{array}{l} \Gamma_{\gamma \beta}^{a} = - \phi^{, a} \eta_{\gamma \beta} + \phi_{, \beta} \delta_{\gamma}^{a} + \phi_{, \gamma} \delta_{\beta}^{a} \\ \Gamma_{\gamma \beta , a}^{a} = - \nabla^{2} \phi \eta_{\gamma \beta} + 2 \phi , \beta \gamma \\ \Gamma_{\gamma \beta}^{a} \Gamma_{\mu a}^{\gamma} = 6 \phi_{, \mu} \phi_{, \beta} - 2 (\nabla \phi )^{2} \eta_{\mu \beta} \\ - \mathbf {g} = \mathbf {e}^{8 \phi}, \quad \log | \mathbf {g} |^{\frac {1}{2}} = 4 \phi \\ \left(\log \left| \mathbf {g} \right|^{\frac {1}{2}}\right)_{, \alpha} \Gamma_{\gamma \beta}^{\alpha} = - 4 (\nabla \phi )^{2} \eta_{\gamma \beta} + 8 \phi , \beta \phi , \gamma \\ \left(\log | \mathbf {g} |^{\frac {1}{2}}\right), \gamma \beta = 4 \phi , \gamma \beta . \\ \end{array}
$$
In the above expressions raising and lowering have been done with $\eta_{\mu 
u}$ , e.g.
$$
\phi^{, a} \equiv \eta^{\mu a} \phi_{, \mu}; \quad \nabla^{2} \phi \equiv \eta^{\mu a} \phi_{, \mu a}; \quad (\nabla \phi )^{2} \equiv \eta^{\mu a} \phi_{, \mu} \phi_{, a}.
$$
The Ricci tensor and scalar are then
$$
\begin{array}{l} \mathrm {R}_{\mu \nu} = - 2 \phi_{, \mu \nu} + 2 \phi_{, \mu} \phi_{, \nu} - \eta_{\mu \nu} [ \nabla^{2} \phi + 2 (\nabla \phi )^{2} ] \\ \mathbf {R} \equiv \mathbf {R}_{\mu}^{\mu} = \mathbf {e}^{- 2 \phi} \eta^{\mu \nu} \mathbf {R}_{\mu \nu} = - 6 \mathbf {e}^{- 2 \phi} [ \nabla^{2} \phi + (\nabla \phi )^{2} ] \\ \end{array}
$$
and the Riemann tensor is
$$
\begin{array}{l} \mathsf {R}_{\alpha \beta \gamma \delta} = \mathsf {C}_{\alpha \beta \gamma \delta} + \frac {1}{2} \left(\mathsf {g}_{\alpha \gamma} \mathsf {R}_{\beta \delta} - \mathsf {g}_{\alpha \delta} \mathsf {R}_{\beta \gamma} - \mathsf {g}_{\beta \gamma} \mathsf {R}_{\alpha \delta} + \mathsf {g}_{\beta \delta} \mathsf {R}_{\alpha \gamma}\right) \\ - \frac {1}{6} \left(\mathbf {g}_{\alpha \gamma} \mathbf {g}_{\beta \delta} - \mathbf {g}_{\alpha \delta} \mathbf {g}_{\beta \gamma}\right) \mathbf {R} \\ = \frac {1}{2} \mathrm {e}^{2 \phi} (\eta_{\alpha \gamma} \mathrm {R}_{\beta \delta} - \eta_{\alpha \delta} \mathrm {R}_{\beta \gamma} - \eta_{\beta \gamma} \mathrm {R}_{\alpha \delta} + \eta_{\beta \delta} \mathrm {R}_{\alpha \gamma}) \\ - \frac {1}{6} \mathrm {e}^{4 \phi} (\eta_{\alpha \gamma} \eta_{\beta \delta} - \eta_{\alpha \delta} \eta_{\beta \gamma}) \mathsf {R} \\ = \mathrm {e}^{2 \phi} [ \eta_{\alpha \gamma} (\phi , \beta \phi , \delta - \phi , \beta \delta) - \eta_{\alpha \delta} (\phi , \beta \phi , \gamma - \phi , \beta \gamma) \\ - \eta_{\beta \gamma} (\phi , a \phi , \delta - \phi , a \delta) + \eta_{\beta \delta} (\phi , a \phi , \gamma - \phi , a \gamma) \\ \left. + (\nabla \phi )^{2} (\vphantom{\int} - \eta_{a \gamma} \eta_{\beta \delta} + \eta_{a \delta} \eta_{\beta \gamma}) ] \right.. \\ \end{array}
$$
origin.solution:
Section 9.19. In a conformally flat spacetime the Weyl tensor vanishes, so the Riemann tensor can easily be computed from the Ricci tensor
$$
\mathrm {R}_{\mu \nu} = - \left(\log | \mathrm {g} |^{\frac {1}{2}}\right)_{, \mu \nu} + \Gamma_{\mu \nu , \alpha}^{\alpha} + \left(\log | \mathrm {g} |^{\frac {1}{2}}\right)_{, \alpha} \Gamma_{\mu \nu}^{\alpha} - \Gamma_{\mu \alpha}^{\beta} \Gamma_{\nu \beta}^{\alpha}.
$$
The expressions we need are
$$
\begin{array}{l} \Gamma_{\gamma \beta}^{a} = - \phi^{, a} \eta_{\gamma \beta} + \phi_{, \beta} \delta_{\gamma}^{a} + \phi_{, \gamma} \delta_{\beta}^{a} \\ \Gamma_{\gamma \beta , a}^{a} = - \nabla^{2} \phi \eta_{\gamma \beta} + 2 \phi , \beta \gamma \\ \Gamma_{\gamma \beta}^{a} \Gamma_{\mu a}^{\gamma} = 6 \phi_{, \mu} \phi_{, \beta} - 2 (\nabla \phi )^{2} \eta_{\mu \beta} \\ - \mathbf {g} = \mathbf {e}^{8 \phi}, \quad \log | \mathbf {g} |^{\frac {1}{2}} = 4 \phi \\ \left(\log \left| \mathbf {g} \right|^{\frac {1}{2}}\right)_{, \alpha} \Gamma_{\gamma \beta}^{\alpha} = - 4 (\nabla \phi )^{2} \eta_{\gamma \beta} + 8 \phi , \beta \phi , \gamma \\ \left(\log | \mathbf {g} |^{\frac {1}{2}}\right), \gamma \beta = 4 \phi , \gamma \beta . \\ \end{array}
$$
In the above expressions raising and lowering have been done with $\eta_{\mu 
u}$ , e.g.
$$
\phi^{, a} \equiv \eta^{\mu a} \phi_{, \mu}; \quad \nabla^{2} \phi \equiv \eta^{\mu a} \phi_{, \mu a}; \quad (\nabla \phi )^{2} \equiv \eta^{\mu a} \phi_{, \mu} \phi_{, a}.
$$
The Ricci tensor and scalar are then
$$
\begin{array}{l} \mathrm {R}_{\mu \nu} = - 2 \phi_{, \mu \nu} + 2 \phi_{, \mu} \phi_{, \nu} - \eta_{\mu \nu} [ \nabla^{2} \phi + 2 (\nabla \phi )^{2} ] \\ \mathbf {R} \equiv \mathbf {R}_{\mu}^{\mu} = \mathbf {e}^{- 2 \phi} \eta^{\mu \nu} \mathbf {R}_{\mu \nu} = - 6 \mathbf {e}^{- 2 \phi} [ \nabla^{2} \phi + (\nabla \phi )^{2} ] \\ \end{array}
$$
and the Riemann tensor is
$$
\begin{array}{l} \mathsf {R}_{\alpha \beta \gamma \delta} = \mathsf {C}_{\alpha \beta \gamma \delta} + \frac {1}{2} \left(\mathsf {g}_{\alpha \gamma} \mathsf {R}_{\beta \delta} - \mathsf {g}_{\alpha \delta} \mathsf {R}_{\beta \gamma} - \mathsf {g}_{\beta \gamma} \mathsf {R}_{\alpha \delta} + \mathsf {g}_{\beta \delta} \mathsf {R}_{\alpha \gamma}\right) \\ - \frac {1}{6} \left(\mathbf {g}_{\alpha \gamma} \mathbf {g}_{\beta \delta} - \mathbf {g}_{\alpha \delta} \mathbf {g}_{\beta \gamma}\right) \mathbf {R} \\ = \frac {1}{2} \mathrm {e}^{2 \phi} (\eta_{\alpha \gamma} \mathrm {R}_{\beta \delta} - \eta_{\alpha \delta} \mathrm {R}_{\beta \gamma} - \eta_{\beta \gamma} \mathrm {R}_{\alpha \delta} + \eta_{\beta \delta} \mathrm {R}_{\alpha \gamma}) \\ - \frac {1}{6} \mathrm {e}^{4 \phi} (\eta_{\alpha \gamma} \eta_{\beta \delta} - \eta_{\alpha \delta} \eta_{\beta \gamma}) \mathsf {R} \\ = \mathrm {e}^{2 \phi} [ \eta_{\alpha \gamma} (\phi , \beta \phi , \delta - \phi , \beta \delta) - \eta_{\alpha \delta} (\phi , \beta \phi , \gamma - \phi , \beta \gamma) \\ - \eta_{\beta \gamma} (\phi , a \phi , \delta - \phi , a \delta) + \eta_{\beta \delta} (\phi , a \phi , \gamma - \phi , a \gamma) \\ \left. + (\nabla \phi )^{2} (\vphantom{\int} - \eta_{a \gamma} \eta_{\beta \delta} + \eta_{a \delta} \eta_{\beta \gamma}) ] \right.. \\ \end{array}
$$
